name: auth
context: Firebase Authentication — email/password, OAuth, phone, anonymous sign-in, custom claims, multi-factor auth, and Admin SDK token management
info: Firebase Authentication provides drop-in UI and SDK methods for authenticating users via email/password, Google, Facebook, Apple, GitHub, and other OAuth providers, phone number SMS verification, and anonymous guest accounts. The client SDK (firebase/auth) exposes createUserWithEmailAndPassword, signInWithEmailAndPassword, signInWithPopup, signInWithPhoneNumber, signInAnonymously, and onAuthStateChanged for reactive session tracking. ID tokens (JWTs) are issued automatically and can be forwarded to backend services for verification. The Admin SDK (firebase-admin) enables server-side operations: verifying ID tokens with verifyIdToken(), minting custom tokens with createCustomToken(), setting custom claims with setCustomUserClaims() for role-based access control, and managing users programmatically. Firebase Security Rules integrate directly with request.auth to gate Firestore, Realtime Database, and Cloud Storage access. Multi-factor authentication adds SMS-based second factors to existing accounts.

sections.title: Email/password authentication
sections.body: Use createUserWithEmailAndPassword() to register new users and signInWithEmailAndPassword() to log in. Both return a UserCredential with the user object. Send verification emails with sendEmailVerification(user). Reset passwords with sendPasswordResetEmail(auth, email). Email enumeration protection can be enabled in the Firebase console to prevent email existence checks.

sections.title: OAuth providers
sections.body: Firebase supports Google, Facebook, Apple, GitHub, Microsoft, Twitter, and Yahoo as built-in OAuth providers. Use signInWithPopup(auth, provider) for popup flow or signInWithRedirect(auth, provider) for full-page redirect. After redirect, call getRedirectResult(auth) to retrieve the credential. Each provider class (GoogleAuthProvider, FacebookAuthProvider, etc.) supports addScope() for requesting additional OAuth scopes. Enable providers in the Firebase console under Authentication > Sign-in method.

sections.title: Phone number authentication
sections.body: Phone auth uses SMS verification with a reCAPTCHA widget for abuse prevention. Call signInWithPhoneNumber(auth, phoneNumber, recaptchaVerifier) to send an SMS code, then confirmationResult.confirm(code) to verify. Phone auth supports linking to existing accounts. Requires enabling Phone provider in the Firebase console. Test phone numbers can be configured to bypass SMS sending during development.

sections.title: Session and state management
sections.body: Use onAuthStateChanged(auth, callback) as the primary way to track auth state. It fires on sign-in, sign-out, and token refresh. auth.currentUser provides synchronous access to the current user but may be null during initialization. ID tokens (JWTs) expire after 1 hour and are auto-refreshed by the SDK. Use getIdToken(true) to force-refresh. Auth persistence can be set to LOCAL (survives browser restart), SESSION (tab only), or NONE (in-memory only) via setPersistence().

sections.title: Admin SDK operations
sections.body: The firebase-admin SDK runs on servers and has elevated privileges. verifyIdToken(idToken) validates client tokens and returns decoded claims. createCustomToken(uid, claims) mints tokens for custom auth systems. getUser(uid), getUserByEmail(email), listUsers() manage user records. deleteUser(uid) removes accounts. The Admin SDK authenticates via a service account JSON file pointed to by GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, or automatically on Google Cloud infrastructure.

sections.title: Custom claims and role-based access
sections.body: Custom claims are key-value pairs attached to a user's ID token via setCustomUserClaims(uid, claims). They propagate to Security Rules as request.auth.token.<claim>. Use for role-based access (admin, editor, viewer). Claims are limited to 1000 bytes total. Changes take effect on the next token refresh (up to 1 hour) unless the client calls getIdToken(true). Do not store large data in claims; use Firestore for user profiles.
